growth_records.strain: Enrichment consortium (propane bioreactor); "Ca. A. nitratireducens" 13.2% relative abundance, "Ca. Brocadia" 2.5-4.9%, "Ca. Kuenenia" 0.1-0.4%

up.method: GC (thermal conductivity detector); rate from concentration profile via linear regression; mean +/- SD 0.35 +/- 0.11
Flow injection analyzer (QuickChem8000); linear regression of concentration profile; mean +/- SD 2.34 +/- 0.30 (as nitrate reduction rate)
Nitrogen balance from reactor performance Day 100-115; mean +/- SD 4.04 +/- 0.28 mmol N/d (combined NO3- + NH4+ consumption)

sec.method: GC-TCD; N-balance Day 100-115; mean +/- SD 4.23 +/- 0.36 mmol N/d
Not quantified as a rate; stoichiometric product of propane oxidation per Eq.1 (C3H8 + 10 NO3- -> 3 CO2 + 10 NO2- + 4 H2O)
Flow injection analyzer; nitrite negligible/not detected during stable operation (scavenged by anammox)

yields.electron_balance_donor_to_acceptor: 1.13 +/- 0.07 (electrons from ammonium+propane oxidation / electrons required for nitrate reduction)

growth_records.cite: Wu, Mengxiong et al. Anaerobic oxidation of ammonium and short-chain gaseous alkanes coupled to nitrate reduction by a bacterial consortium. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae063. [PMC11090206]
growth_records.snippet: propane oxidation and nitrate reduction rates in the current propane bioreactor (0.35 ± 0.11 and 2.34 ± 0.30 mmol/L/d, respectively) were ~ 17-21 times higher than our previous systems without anammox bacteria (0.02 ± 0.01 and 0.11 ± 0.04 m
growth_records.notes: Volumetric (not biomass-specific) rates; no gDW/OD reported so qS/mmol-gDW-h and mu not derivable. Propane uptake 0.35 +/- 0.11 mmol/L/d and nitrate reduction 2.34 +/- 0.30 mmol/L/d are directly measured in THIS paper (linear regression of concentration profiles, Fig.1/Supp Table 1). N2 production 4.23 +/- 0.36 mmol N/d and total NO3-+NH4+ consumption 4.04 +/- 0.28 mmol N/d are the N-balance values (Fig.1B). Comparison rates 0.02 +/- 0.01 (propane) and 0.11 +/- 0.04 mmol/L/d (nitrate) are CITED from the authors' previous system (ref.10) and are NOT measured here. Nitrite not detected during stable operation. Temperature recorded as room temperature 22 +/- 2 C; pH not stated. Medium is 'anoxic synthetic medium' referencing ref.10 - composition not given in this article.

growth_records.strain: Enrichment consortium (butane bioreactor); "Ca. A. nitratireducens" 5.8% relative abundance, "Ca. Brocadia" 0.3-1.4%, "Ca. Kuenenia" 3.7-4.1%

up.method: GC-MS; rate from concentration profile via linear regression; mean +/- SD 0.24 +/- 0.10
Flow injection analyzer (QuickChem8000); linear regression; mean +/- SD 2.06 +/- 0.33 (nitrate reduction rate)
Nitrogen balance; mean +/- SD 3.62 +/- 0.41 mmol N/d (combined NO3- + NH4+ consumption)

sec.method: GC-TCD; N-balance; mean +/- SD 3.62 +/- 0.31 mmol N/d
Flow injection analyzer; nitrite not detected during stable operation (scavenged by anammox)

yields.electron_balance_donor_to_acceptor: 1.13 +/- 0.12 (electrons from ammonium+butane oxidation / electrons required for nitrate reduction)

growth_records.cite: Wu, Mengxiong et al. Anaerobic oxidation of ammonium and short-chain gaseous alkanes coupled to nitrate reduction by a bacterial consortium. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae063. [PMC11090206]
growth_records.snippet: butane and nitrate consumption rates also increased from 0.09 ± 0.06 and 0.58 ± 0.30 mmol/L/d in previous systems to 0.24 ± 0.10 and 2.06 ± 0.33 mmol/L/d in this butane bioreactor, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Volumetric (not biomass-specific) rates; no gDW/OD so qS and mu not derivable. Butane uptake 0.24 +/- 0.10 mmol/L/d and nitrate reduction 2.06 +/- 0.33 mmol/L/d directly measured in THIS paper. N2 production 3.62 +/- 0.31 mmol N/d and total NO3-+NH4+ consumption 3.62 +/- 0.41 mmol N/d from N-balance. Comparison rates 0.09 +/- 0.06 (butane) and 0.58 +/- 0.30 mmol/L/d (nitrate) are CITED from authors' previous system (ref.11), not measured here. Temperature room temperature 22 +/- 2 C; pH not stated; medium composition references ref.10.
